---
title: "The interactive app"
toc: false
---

The tutorial app lets you climb every rung of the ladder across four datasets —
with **live** [causal discovery](workflow/01-causal-discovery.qmd) and
[simulation validation](workflow/04-validation.qmd), not just precomputed views.
It runs locally from the repository.

::: {.callout-note}
## Why local, not a hosted app

The interactive lab ships as pinned, runnable source rather than a hosted service —
a deliberate reproducibility choice, not a limitation. A public app instance can't
guarantee the compute environment behind published results: dependencies drift,
free tiers sleep, hosts get retired. A pinned repository reproduces the exact
numbers indefinitely. The full workflow also depends on scientific Python packages
(`torch` for the CVAE generator, `causal-learn`, `xgboost`, `shap`) that don't run
in a browser (WebAssembly) sandbox — so running from the repo is the only place the
*complete* workflow actually runs.
:::

## Run it

```bash
git clone https://github.com/andystats/causal-shap-target-dags
cd causal-shap-target-dags/app
pip install -r requirements.txt
shiny run app.py            # then open http://localhost:8000
```

Jump straight to a rung with a query string: `…/?stage=1` (discovery),
`?stage=4` (validation), and so on.

The app is **torch-free by default** — discovery uses `causal-learn` and live
validation uses a moment-matched multivariate-normal generator, both fast. The
heavier paths (CVAE training, NOTEARS) live in the build scripts
(`python -m causal_shap.build …`), which regenerate the frozen bundles the app reads.
